perturbation.original_text: The parties hereby agree to submit all disputes hereunder to arbitration in Connecticut, USA in accordance with the rules of the International Chamber of Commerce . All arbitration proceedings shall be conducted exclusively in the English language before a single arbitrator. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if SUPPLIER is alleged to have breached this Agreement, then GK shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties hereby agree to submit all disputes hereunder in accordance with the rules of the International Chamber of Commerce . All arbitration proceedings shall be conducted exclusively in the English language before a single arbitrator. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if SUPPLIER is alleged to have breached this Agreement, then GK shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief from any court of competent jurisdiction. The location of arbitration is subject to GK's approval.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the location of arbitration determination to GK's approval after stating it will be in Connecticut makes the clause ambiguous and potentially unenforceable due to uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate
perturbation.law_explanation: By stating Connecticut, USA as the location of arbitration and then stating that the location is subject to GK's approval, it could be argued that the arbitration agreement is not 'valid, irrevocable, and enforceable' under 9 U.S. Code § 2 because the terms are now ambiguous. This introduces uncertainty as to where arbitration will occur.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate U.S. Code Notes prev | next A written provision in any maritime transaction or a contract evidencing a transaction involving commerce to settle by arbitration a controversy thereafter arising out of such contract or transaction, or the refusal to perform the whole or any part thereof, or an agreement in writing to submit to arbitration an existing controversy arising out of such a contract, transaction, or refusal, shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract or as otherwise provided in chapter 4. (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 670 ; Pub. L. 117–90, § 2(b)(1)(A) , Mar. 3, 2022 , 136 Stat. 27 .) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, § 2, 43 Stat. 883 . Editorial Notes Amendments 2022— Pub. L. 117–90 inserted “or as otherwise provided in chapter 4” before period at end. Statutory Notes and Related Subsidiaries Effective Date of 2022 Amendment Amendment by Pub. L. 117–90 applicable with respect to any dispute or claim that arises or accrues on or after Mar. 3, 2022 , see section 3 of Pub. L. 117–90 , set out as an Effective Date note under section 401 of this title . U.S. Code Toolbox Law about... perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a structural flaw by creating an ambiguity regarding the location of arbitration, contradicting the requirement for a valid and enforceable agreement under 9 U.S. Code § 2. The original text specifies Connecticut, USA, while the changed text allows GK to approve the location, creating uncertainty. As 9 U.S. Code § 2 states that agreements to arbitrate shall be 'valid, irrevocable, and enforceable,' the ambiguity introduced undermines the agreement's enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement sets out and constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the disclosure, protection and use of Confidential Information and supersedes all prior agreements, whether oral or written, relating to such matters and all other prior communications between the pa rties relating to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement sets out and constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the disclosure, protection and use of Confidential Information. SUPPLIER shall be responsible for all attorney fees related to disputes under this agreement. This supersedes all prior agreements, whether oral or written, relating to such matters and all other prior communications between the pa rties relating to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Inserting a clause about SUPPLIER being responsible for all attorney fees in the 'entire agreement' section creates ambiguity and potential unfairness, as it's not clearly identified as a dispute resolution term. This might be deemed unconscionable under general contract law principles.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Connecticut General Statutes § 42-133dd. Unconscionable agreement or clause.
perturbation.law_explanation: Inserting a clause requiring the supplier to pay all legal fees in what is presented as an entire agreement clause instead of a dispute resolution clause could be argued as being an unconscionable agreement under Connecticut General Statutes § 42-133dd, because it is being buried in the middle of another provision.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation introduces a clause about the SUPPLIER being responsible for all attorney fees. This addition, within what's presented as the 'entire agreement' section, rather than a dedicated dispute resolution section, creates ambiguity and potential unfairness. The Law Explanation suggests that burying such a clause could render it unconscionable under Connecticut General Statutes § 42-133dd. Given the definition of 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction', where a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret, the placement of the attorney fees clause creates a structural flaw that contradicts legal norms.

perturbation.original_text: SUPPLIER agrees to hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not disclose or use the Confidential Information for its benefit or the benefit of any other company or entity anywhere in the world or any other purpose other than for the Purpose set forth in the recitals herein.
perturbation.changed_text: SUPPLIER agrees to hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not disclose or use the Confidential Information for its benefit or the benefit of any other company or entity anywhere in the world or any other purpose other than for the Purpose set forth in the recitals herein. SUPPLIER may disclose confidential information with GK's written permission.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'SUPPLIER may disclose confidential information with GK's written permission' after a strict confidentiality clause creates a contradiction and undermines the confidentiality requirement. It introduces uncertainty about the extent of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 163 - When a Misrepresentation Prevents Formation of a Contract
perturbation.law_explanation: By adding an allowance for Supplier to use confidential information, the terms become contradictory and could potentially impact the validity of the formation of the contract. The addition of the allowance can lead to confusion about the terms of the contract, which impacts the formation.

perturbation.justification: The original text establishes a strict confidentiality obligation on the Supplier, prohibiting disclosure or use of Confidential Information except for the specific Purpose defined in the contract. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing the Supplier to disclose confidential information with GK's written permission. This directly contradicts the strict confidentiality requirement established in the original clause, as it creates a situation where the Supplier can now disclose confidential information, which was previously prohibited. The Law Explanation also states that the terms become contradictory which can impact the validity of the formation of the contract. While the scraped law snippets are unhelpful, the contradiction is clear based on the original and modified text and the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Any disclosure of Confidential Inform ation shall be limited SUPPLIER’s employees and who have a need to use or study such Confidential Information for the Purpose. SUPPLIER shall advise its employees and agen ts of its obligations pursuant to this Agreement, but SUPPLIER shall remain responsible to ensure their compliance.
perturbation.changed_text: Any disclosure of Confidential Inform ation shall be limited SUPPLIER’s employees and who have a need to use or study such Confidential Information for the Purpose. SUPPLIER shall advise its employees and agen ts of its obligations pursuant to this Agreement. It is recommended but not required that SUPPLIER shall remain responsible to ensure their compliance.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'SUPPLIER shall remain responsible to ensure their compliance' to 'It is recommended but not required that SUPPLIER shall remain responsible to ensure their compliance' weakens the supplier's responsibility for employee compliance. This weakens the confidential information protection.
perturbation.law_explanation: By weakening the Supplier's responsibility for employee compliance, it hinders the company's ability to take action against any misuse of the trade secrets because of the difficulty in proving it, undermining the protection of trade secrets as per UTSA § 3(a).

perturbation.justification: The modification changes a mandatory obligation ('SUPPLIER shall remain responsible') to a recommendation ('It is recommended but not required that SUPPLIER shall remain responsible'). This weakens the supplier's responsibility for employee compliance regarding confidential information, potentially undermining trade secret protection, which the LLM explanation links to UTSA § 3(a). This falls under the definition of a structural flaw that creates a legal contradiction by weakening enforceability of a compliance obligation. The modified clause would likely fail legal scrutiny because it transforms a requirement into a suggestion.
